## Grove commit discipline

Before you exit, commit your work. Do not print a summary and leave — that work is lost when the worktree is cleaned up.

```
git add -A -- ':!node_modules' ':!.env*'
git commit -m '<type>(<task-id>): <subject>'   # e.g. feat(P8-A1): …
git log -1                                      # verify the commit landed
```

`<task-id>` is the PLAN.md identifier from the spec you were given (P8-A1, P4-API-2, CLI-A3, REST-2, etc.). A successful commit with that ID in the subject triggers the PostToolUse hook which auto-marks PLAN.md — you do not edit PLAN.md by hand.

## Cross-repo: grove-www

If your work touches `/Users/jm/src/grove-www/`, checkout main and sync before editing:

```
git -C /Users/jm/src/grove-www checkout main
git -C /Users/jm/src/grove-www pull origin main --ff-only
```

Then make changes and commit there too, with the same `<type>(<task-id>):` convention. grove-www has no branch protection — direct push is fine. Never leave changes on a stale feature branch.

## Non-negotiables

- DO NOT EXIT WITHOUT COMMITTING.
- DO NOT push to `main` on the grove repo — open a PR. grove-www is direct-push OK.
- DO NOT edit PLAN.md or docs/phases-shipped.md by hand — the marker hook handles this.
